title: lepisorus maculosus
intro: Lepisorus maculosus, also known as the spotted leather fern, is a species of fern native to Asia and parts of the Himalayan region. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Lepisorus maculosus is used in various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
